feed.title: Submission of varops budget and tapscript leaf 0xc2 BIPS (aka "Script Restoration") for BIP numbers.

entry.title: Submission of varops budget and tapscript leaf 0xc2 BIPS (aka "Script Restoration") for BIP numbers.
entry.summary:
The recent submission of a Pull Request (PR) to the Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs) repository marks a significant step in the evolution of Bitcoin scripting capabilities. This PR encompasses the first two drafts of the "A Bitcoin Scripting Proposal BIP Quartet," which have been previously discussed and can be found at [https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2118](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2118). A noteworthy update from the last discussion is the increase in costs for certain operations, specifically hashing and copying bytes. This adjustment stems from extensive benchmarking across a broader spectrum of machines, reflecting a strategic move to ensure that worst-case validation times remain manageable across all viable hardware platforms. Such a conservative approach underscores the commitment to maintaining operational integrity without compromising on performance.
Despite this progress, it's important to acknowledge that the remaining two BIPs, namely OP_TX and new opcodes, have not yet been submitted. The primary reason behind this decision is their current status; they are mainly instrumental in outlining what functional gaps persist following the proposed script extensions, rather than being ready for full-scale implementation. This phased approach indicates a methodical and structured roadmap towards enhancing Bitcoin's scripting functionalities while ensuring each step is thoroughly vetted and beneficial.
In addition to providing technical updates, this communication serves as an integral touchpoint with the developer community, fostering transparency and collaboration. It reflects an ongoing dialogue among developers to refine and enhance Bitcoin's infrastructure, reinforcing the collective endeavor to advance the cryptocurrency's technological foundation.
